title: DB migration v6: add spendable note plaintext columns
description:
The current notes table stores balance/history metadata but not the note plaintext needed to reconstruct a spendable sapling::Note for the tx builder.

Add a DB migration v6 that adds three nullable columns to the notes table:
  note_g_d      BLOB     -- 11-byte Sapling diversifier (g_d)
  note_pk_d     BLOB     -- 32-byte recipient public key (pk_d)
  note_rseed    BLOB     -- 32-byte commitment randomness (rseed, new format) OR 32-byte rcm (old)
  note_rseed_after_zip212  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1  -- 1 if rseed, 0 if old rcm format

These columns are NULL for existing notes (pre-decryption) and populated when a note is successfully decrypted by the scanner.

Also add a new query:
  pub async fn spendable_notes(&self, account: u32) -> Result<Vec<SpendableNote>>
  where SpendableNote = { note_id, value_zatoshi, note_g_d, note_pk_d, note_rseed, rseed_after_zip212, block_height }

Defensive programming rules:
1. If note_g_d/note_pk_d/note_rseed is NULL, exclude the note from spendable_notes() — never return a note that cannot be reconstructed
2. The migration must be idempotent (test: apply twice, no error)
3. Test: existing note rows survive migration with NULL plaintext columns
4. Test: spendable_notes() excludes notes with NULL plaintext columns
5. Test: spendable_notes() includes notes where all three columns are populated

Use a subagent to read the existing migrations in db.rs (find the current highest version) before writing migration v6. Never modify existing migrations.
acceptance_criteria: migration v6 runs; spendable_notes() returns only notes with all plaintext columns populated; existing migration tests still pass
